title: horkelia cuneata subsp. puberula
intro: Horkelia cuneata subsp. puberula, also known as the puberula horkelia, is a plant species in the heath family (Ericaceae) that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Horkelia cuneata subsp. puberula include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its antimicrobial properties which can help protect against infections. Horkelia cuneata subsp. puberula can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
